revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:稲佐の浜]][[ko:이나사노하마]]
{{ill|Inasa Beach|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|qq=}}
{{Infobox geographical feature|name = {{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|qq=}} | image = [[File:Bentenjima on Inasa Beach.jpg|thumb|Bentenjima at Inasa Beach]] | image2 = [[File:Inasanohama.JPG|thumb|Bentenjima and its surroundings]] | location = {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|qid=Q825166|lt=Izumo, Shimane|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|24|2.9|N|132|40|19.7|E|display=title}} | type = {{ill|Beach|en|Beach|qid=Q40080|lt=Beach|1=Beach}} | length = ~10 km | access = Public }}
'''{{ill|Inasa Beach|ja|稲佐の浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inasa Beach|qq=}}''' is a beach located in {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|qid=Q825166|lt=Izumo, Shimane|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, Japan. It stretches approximately 10 km along the coast, curving in an arc toward the west.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Story of Inasa Beach|url=https://www.city.izumo.shimane.jp/www/contents/1493026512141/files/story.pdf|website={{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|qid=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}}|accessdate=2023-12-28}}</ref>
== Overview == Inasa Beach is the legendary site of the {{ill|Kuniyuzuri myth|ja|葦原中国平定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuniyuzuri myth|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621119}}, where the land of Japan was ceded to the heavenly deities. It is referred to as '''Inasa-no-Kohama''' (伊那佐之小濱) in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}<ref>{{Cite book|title=Kojiki (Original Text)|publisher=Unknown|page=Volume 1, Section 5}}</ref> and '''Itosa-no-Kohama''' (五十田狭の小汀) in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.
Additionally, the southern extension of Inasa Beach forms the western coastline of {{ill|Shimane Peninsula|ja|島根半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Peninsula|1=Shimane Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11052312}}, known as '''Sono-no-Nagahama''' (薗の長浜). According to the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it was regarded as the "rope" that connects the Shimane Peninsula to {{ill|Mount Sanbe|en|Mount Sanbe|qid=Q4179256|lt=Mount Sanbe|1=Mount Sanbe}}, playing a role in the {{ill|Kunibiki myth|ja|国引き神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunibiki myth|1=Kunibiki-shinwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420924}}.
While the beach is used for swimming in the summer, past incidents have led to concerns over safety. The shoreline contains hidden trenches and strong {{ill|Rip current|en|Rip current|qid=Q898375|lt=Rip currents|1=Rip current}}, making it hazardous. In 2024, the Sakaiminato Coast Guard issued warnings advising people not to swim in restricted areas.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://news.ntv.co.jp/n/nkt/category/society/nkd89874b31a5c468db66d3982f11e367a |title="Do Not Swim in Restricted Areas" – Inasa Beach |publisher=Nihonkai TV |date=2024-08-08 |accessdate=2024-10-27}}</ref>
== Events == Inasa Beach is closely associated with the rituals of {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|qid=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha|1=Izumo-taisha}}, where the following ceremonies take place: * '''Shinkōsai (神幸祭)''' – August 14 * '''Kamimukaesai (神迎祭)''' – The 10th day of the 10th lunar month
== Landmarks == * '''Bentenjima (弁天島)''' – A small islet at the center of Inasa Beach. It originally housed a shrine to {{ill|Benzaiten|en|Benzaiten|qid=Q818468|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}}, but now enshrines {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|豊玉毘古命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579231|1=Watatsumi}}. * '''Shiokaki-jima (塩掻島)''' – During the '''Shinkōsai''', salt is drawn from this island and offered at {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|qid=Q696362|lt=Izumo Taisha|1=Izumo-taisha}}. * '''Byōbu-iwa (屏風岩)''' – The rock where {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} and {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} negotiated the transfer of the land to the heavenly deities. * '''Tsubute-iwa (つぶて岩)''' – According to legend, this rock was thrown during a test of strength between {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and Takemikazuchi during the Kuniyuzuri myth.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Inasa Beach}} * {{ill|100 Best Beaches in Japan|ja|日本の渚百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Best Beaches in Japan|qq=}} – Inasa Beach is listed. * {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}} * {{ill|Cape Hinomisaki|ja|日御碕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cape Hinomisaki|1=Cape Hinomisaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505328}}
== External Links == * [https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20281 Inasa Beach] - {{ill|Shimane Prefecture Tourism Federation|ja|島根県観光連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane Prefecture Tourism Federation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimane Tourism Navi) * [https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/20386 Inasa Beach Swimming Area] - Shimane Tourism Navi * [https://www.izumo-kankou.gr.jp/213 Inasa Beach] - {{ill|Izumo Tourism Association|ja|出雲観光協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Tourism Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [http://museum-database.shimane-u.ac.jp/marugoto/238 Inasa Beach] - {{ill|Shimane University Museum|ja|島根まるごとミュージアム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimane University Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{Japan-geo-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|稲佐の浜|version=103956327|comment=Imported full ja history}}
